_comment_2: Composes the canonical p02-jwt + p04-rl-static + p08-req-headers + p09-resp-headers + p10-req-body + p11-resp-body axes into a single Tyk Classic API definition. Every primitive is the exact same one already validated in its own profile; nothing here is novel except the ordering rules that come for free from Tyk's middleware chain.
_comment_3: Documented Tyk middleware order (v5.11.1, gateway/api_loader.go): VersionCheck -> RequestSizeLimit -> JSVM `pre` (custom_middleware.pre) -> JWT auth (mw_jwt.go) -> RateCheckMW (global_rate_limit, dispatched as RateLimitForAPI in mw_api_rate_limit.go) -> RateLimitAndQuotaCheck (per-session) -> TransformMiddleware (extended_paths.transform) -> TransformHeaders (extended_paths.transform_headers) -> reverse proxy -> response chain (transform_response_headers + transform_response). Probe 1 (valid JWT, full body) walks every stage; probes 2/3 short-circuit at JWT; probe 4 (1200 rps burst) saturates RateLimitForAPI before headers/body transforms.
_comment_4: Body rewrite uses Tyk's NATIVE `transform` middleware (Go template + bundled Sprig v3 FuncMap), NOT the otto JSVM `pre` middleware p11 originally tried. The JSVM path adds enough per-request overhead (MiniRequestObject JSON (un)marshal + otto VM context switch) to cap throughput at ~830 rps, below the 1000-rps `global_rate_limit` threshold, so the burst bucket never fills and 0 x 429 results. Native `transform` runs in-process Go (no VM, no JSON envelope) and the burst probe lands the expected 200 +/- 50 x 429. The Sprig template is shared with p10-req-body at _shared/templates/p10_request_rewrite.tmpl.
_comment_5: transform / transform_headers / transform_response_headers / transform_response are all method-keyed via gorilla/mux URLSpec wildcards. The fixture exercises POST (probe 1, body) and GET (probe 4, burst). transform is registered ONLY for POST — Tyk's mw_transform.go reads/parses the body unconditionally on a match, and registering it for GET would force a body-parse round-trip on every burst hop for no semantic gain (the GET probes carry no body and have no body contract). Header transforms are registered for both methods because both probes assert `X-Bench-In: 1` on the upstream-echoed headers (probe 1 directly, probe 4 implicitly via the keep-`200` path). Response transforms are likewise registered for both methods because probe 1 asserts `X-Bench-Out` + body rewrite on the response, and the burst probe's 1000 successful 2xx hops also benefit from response-shape consistency.
_comment_6: Cosmetic FAIL warning: probe 2 (missing Authorization) expects 401 but Tyk returns 400 (mw_jwt.go's `Authorization field missing` branch hard-codes http.StatusBadRequest). This is the same deviation already documented in p02-jwt/NOTES.md and is not overridable in Tyk Classic OSS without a custom build. Probes 1, 3, 4 land cleanly: 3/4 PASS expected.
name: bench
